sentences.base_language: My recipe for paella
sentences.target_language: 我的西班牙海鲜饭食谱
sentences.romanized: wǒ de xībānyá hǎixiān fàn shípǔ
sentences.grammarNote: **食谱** (shípǔ) = recipe: **食** (shí, food/eat) + **谱** (pǔ, chart/score/guide). The same **谱** appears in **乐谱** (music score). **西班牙** (Xībānyá) is the Chinese name for Spain — a phonetic transliteration. **海鲜饭** (hǎixiān fàn) = seafood rice: **海鲜** (seafood: sea + fresh) + **饭** (cooked rice/meal).

sentences.base_language: One of my favorite foods is paella
sentences.target_language: 我最喜欢的食物之一是西班牙海鲜饭
sentences.romanized: wǒ zuì xǐhuān de shíwù zhī yī shì xībānyá hǎixiān fàn
sentences.grammarNote: **…之一** (zhī yī) = 'one of…': **最喜欢的食物之一** = 'one of my favorite foods.' **之** is a classical/literary possessive/connective particle (like **的** but more formal). This pattern is common in both written and spoken Chinese for expressing 'one of the [superlative] things.' **最喜欢的** = most-liked (superlative with **最**).

sentences.base_language: Traditionally, paella is made with sea food but My recipe is vegetarian
sentences.target_language: 传统上，西班牙海鲜饭是用海鲜做的，但我的食谱是素食的
sentences.romanized: chuántǒng shàng, xībānyá hǎixiān fàn shì yòng hǎixiān zuò de, dàn wǒ de shípǔ shì sùshí de
sentences.grammarNote: 是...做的 (shì...zuò de) is a pattern for explaining how something was made: 是用海鲜做的 = 'is made using seafood'.

sentences.base_language: First I cut half a yellow onion,
sentences.target_language: 首先我切半个黄洋葱，
sentences.romanized: shǒuxiān wǒ qiē bàn gè huáng yángcōng,
sentences.grammarNote: **切** (qiē) = to cut/slice (food). Common cooking verbs: **切** (slice), **剁** (mince by chopping), **削** (peel/pare). **半个** (bàn gè) = half of one — **半** (half) comes before the measure word: **半个苹果** (half an apple), **半杯水** (half a glass of water). **洋葱** (yángcōng) = onion: **洋** (overseas/foreign) + **葱** (green onion) — literally 'foreign onion.'

sentences.base_language: I mince four cloves of garlic,
sentences.target_language: 我切碎四瓣大蒜，
sentences.romanized: wǒ qiē suì sì bàn dàsuàn,
sentences.grammarNote: **切碎** (qiē suì) = mince/chop finely: **切** (cut) + **碎** (broken into pieces) — a verb + result complement. **碎** (suì) as a complement means 'to a smashed or finely cut state.' **瓣** (bàn) is the measure word for cloves of garlic and segments of fruit: **一瓣大蒜** (a clove of garlic), **一瓣橙子** (a segment of orange). **大蒜** (dàsuàn) = garlic: **大** (big) + **蒜** (garlic plant).

sentences.base_language: And I chop 2 small carrots and a bell pepper
sentences.target_language: 然后我切两根小胡萝卜和一个甜椒
sentences.romanized: ránhòu wǒ qiē liǎng gēn xiǎo húluóbo hé yī gè tiánjiāo
sentences.grammarNote: **根** (gēn) is the measure word for long, thin objects: carrots, bananas, ropes, candles, bones. **两根** = two (of something long and thin). **胡萝卜** (húluóbo) = carrot: **胡** (foreign/from the north/west) + **萝卜** (radish) — literally 'foreign radish.' **甜椒** (tiánjiāo) = bell pepper: **甜** (sweet) + **椒** (pepper). Compare **辣椒** (lājiāo, chili pepper — hot pepper).

sentences.base_language: Everything is cooked in one pan so I start heating up some oil on the stove
sentences.target_language: 所有东西都在一个锅里煮，所以我先在炉子上加热一些油
sentences.romanized: suǒyǒu dōngxi dōu zài yī gè guō lǐ zhǔ, suǒyǐ wǒ xiān zài lúzi shàng jiārè yīxiē yóu
sentences.grammarNote: **所有…都** (suǒyǒu…dōu) = 'all…every': **所有** (all/every) + **都** (all, before the verb) is a common pairing for totality. **锅里** = inside the pot: **锅** (guō, wok/pot) + **里** (inside). **先** (xiān) = first/to start — implies a sequence: **先…再** (first… then). **加热** (jiārè) = heat up: **加** (add) + **热** (hot) → make hot.

sentences.base_language: Cook the onion and garlic until the onion is translucent
sentences.target_language: 炒洋葱和大蒜直到洋葱变透明
sentences.romanized: chǎo yángcōng hé dàsuàn zhídào yángcōng biàn tòumíng
sentences.grammarNote: **炒** (chǎo) = stir-fry. One of the most important Chinese cooking verbs: **炒** (stir-fry), **煮** (boil), **蒸** (steam), **烤** (roast), **炸** (deep-fry). **直到** (zhídào) = until/up to the point that: **直** (straight/all the way) + **到** (reach). **变** (biàn) = become/change into — a versatile change-of-state verb: **变透明** (become transparent), **变冷** (get cold), **变好** (get better).

sentences.base_language: Then you add the carrots, bell pepper, and a can of artichokes
sentences.target_language: 然后加入胡萝卜、甜椒和一罐洋蓟
sentences.romanized: ránhòu jiārù húluóbo, tiánjiāo hé yī guàn yángjì
sentences.grammarNote: **加入** (jiārù) = add in/incorporate: **加** (add) + **入** (enter/into). A slightly more formal cooking term compared to just **加** (add). **罐** (guàn) is the measure word for canned or jarred goods: **一罐** (a can/jar). **洋蓟** (yángjì) = artichoke: **洋** (foreign) + **蓟** (thistle-like plant) — another **洋** food word meaning an imported ingredient.

sentences.base_language: After ten minutes add half a can of tomato paste
sentences.target_language: 十分钟后加入半罐番茄酱
sentences.romanized: shí fēnzhōng hòu jiārù bàn guàn fānqié jiàng
sentences.grammarNote: **十分钟后** = after ten minutes: time expression + **后** (after/later) creates a time clause. **番茄酱** (fānqié jiàng) = tomato paste/sauce: **番茄** (tomato: **番** foreign + **茄** eggplant-family plant) + **酱** (jiàng, paste/sauce). **酱** is a key culinary suffix: **酱油** (soy sauce), **豆瓣酱** (chili bean paste), **花生酱** (peanut butter).

sentences.base_language: Season with black pepper, paprika, salt, and just a pinch of saffron (optional)
sentences.target_language: 用黑胡椒、辣椒粉、盐调味，加一点藏红花（可选）
sentences.romanized: yòng hēi hújiāo, làjiāo fěn, yán tiáowèi, jiā yīdiǎn zànghónghuā (kěxuǎn)
sentences.grammarNote: **调味** (tiáowèi) = to season / adjust flavour: **调** (adjust/harmonize) + **味** (taste/flavour). Used as a verb: 用盐调味 = season with salt. **藏红花** (zànghónghuā) = saffron: **藏** (Tibetan/hidden) + **红花** (red flower) — 'the red flower from Tibet.' **可选** (kěxuǎn) = optional: **可** (can/may) + **选** (choose). **一点** (yīdiǎn) = a little bit (after a verb or before a noun).

sentences.base_language: Evenly cover
sentences.target_language: 均匀覆盖
sentences.romanized: jūnyún fùgài
sentences.grammarNote: **均匀** (jūnyún) = evenly/uniformly: **均** (equal) + **匀** (even/uniform) — both characters reinforce the idea of even distribution. Used for spreading spices, rice, etc. **覆盖** (fùgài) = to cover (a surface): **覆** (overturn/cover over) + **盖** (lid/cover). Compare **盖上** (put a lid on), **盖子** (lid).

sentences.base_language: After all that mixes in well add two and a half cups of vegeatable broth
sentences.target_language: 等这些都混合好后，加入两杯半蔬菜汤
sentences.romanized: děng zhèxiē dōu hùnhé hǎo hòu, jiārù liǎng bēi bàn shūcài tāng
sentences.grammarNote: **等…后** = 'after waiting for … to happen.' **等** here is not 'wait' for time, but a subordinating particle: 'once these are well mixed.' **混合好** = mix well: **混合** (mix) + **好** (result complement: done properly). **两杯半** = two and a half cups — in Chinese, fractions come after the measure word: **两杯半** (two-and-a-half cups) rather than 'two and a half cups.'

sentences.base_language: If you want it to be more like traditional paella you would add chicken broth
sentences.target_language: 如果你想要更传统的口味，可以加鸡汤
sentences.romanized: rúguǒ nǐ xiǎng yào gèng chuántǒng de kǒuwèi, kěyǐ jiā jītāng
sentences.grammarNote: **如果…可以…** = 'If you want…, you can…' — **如果** (rúguǒ) introduces a condition, **可以** gives the permitted option. **口味** (kǒuwèi) = taste/flavour profile: **口** (mouth) + **味** (taste). **鸡汤** (jītāng) = chicken broth: **鸡** (chicken) + **汤** (soup/broth). **汤** is also used for many soups: **排骨汤** (pork rib soup), **海鲜汤** (seafood soup).

sentences.base_language: Bring the broth up to a boil then add bomba rice evenly
sentences.target_language: 把汤煮沸，然后均匀加入炸弹米
sentences.romanized: bǎ tāng zhǔ fèi, ránhòu jūnyún jiārù zhàdàn mǐ
sentences.grammarNote: 把汤煮沸 uses the 把 structure to show what happens to the object: 'take the broth and boil it to boiling point'.

sentences.base_language: Cook on high for 5 minutes
sentences.target_language: 大火煮5分钟
sentences.romanized: dà huǒ zhǔ wǔ fēnzhōng
sentences.grammarNote: **大火** (dà huǒ) = high heat (literally 'big fire'). Chinese cooking uses a three-level heat system: **大火** (high heat), **中火** (medium heat), **小火** (low heat/gentle simmer). This reflects the importance of wok cooking where heat level is crucial. **煮** (zhǔ) = boil/cook in liquid.

sentences.base_language: Cook on medium for 5 minutes
sentences.target_language: 中火煮5分钟
sentences.romanized: zhōng huǒ zhǔ wǔ fēnzhōng
sentences.grammarNote: **中火** (zhōng huǒ) = medium heat: **中** (middle) + **火** (fire). **中** in Chinese cooking and daily life often signals the balanced middle option: **中等** (medium grade), **中号** (medium size). Duration follows the verb directly: **煮五分钟** = 'cook for five minutes' (no preposition needed).

sentences.base_language: Let the paella cook for another fifteen minutes on low (gently simmer)
sentences.target_language: 让海鲜饭用小火再煮十五分钟（慢慢炖）
sentences.romanized: ràng hǎixiān fàn yòng xiǎo huǒ zài zhǔ shíwǔ fēnzhōng (mànmàn dùn)
sentences.grammarNote: **让** (ràng) = let/allow (causative): **让海鲜饭再煮** = 'let the paella cook further.' **小火** = low heat/gentle simmer. **再** (zài) = again/more/another — used for future or continuing actions: **再煮十五分钟** (cook for another fifteen minutes). **慢慢炖** = slowly stew: **炖** (dùn) = to stew/braise slowly, distinct from **煮** (boil rapidly).

sentences.base_language: You can add shrimp at this stage if you want some sort of meat
sentences.target_language: 如果你想加肉，这时候可以加虾
sentences.romanized: rúguǒ nǐ xiǎng jiā ròu, zhè shíhou kěyǐ jiā xiā
sentences.grammarNote: **这时候** (zhè shíhou) = at this point/at this time — useful for recipe instructions to indicate the stage you're at. **虾** (xiā) = shrimp/prawn. Seafood vocabulary: **虾** (shrimp), **鱼** (yú, fish), **螃蟹** (pángxiè, crab), **贝类** (bèilèi, shellfish). **加肉** = add meat — **肉** (ròu) refers to meat in general, or specifically pork in context (the default meat in Chinese cooking).

sentences.base_language: I like to burn it a little at the end by going back to high for one minute
sentences.target_language: 我喜欢最后用大火烧一分钟，让它稍微焦一点
sentences.romanized: wǒ xǐhuān zuìhòu yòng dà huǒ shāo yī fēnzhōng, ràng tā shāowēi jiāo yīdiǎn
sentences.grammarNote: **最后** (zuìhòu) = at the end / finally (for the last step). **稍微** (shāowēi) = slightly / just a little: a softer alternative to **有点** (yǒudiǎn). **焦** (jiāo) = burnt/caramelized — in paella, the crispy bottom layer is called **锅巴** (guōbā) in Chinese and is highly prized. **稍微焦一点** = 'just slightly charred/caramelized.'

sentences.base_language: Take off the stove, cover and let rest for ten minutes
sentences.target_language: 离火，盖上盖子静置十分钟
sentences.romanized: lí huǒ, gài shàng gàizi jìngzhì shí fēnzhōng
sentences.grammarNote: **离火** (lí huǒ) = remove from heat: **离** (lí, leave/separate from) + **火** (fire/heat). **离** is used for leaving or distancing from a place or source. **盖上盖子** = put the lid on: **盖上** (cover + directional **上**) + **盖子** (lid). **静置** (jìngzhì) = let rest/stand: **静** (still/quiet) + **置** (place/set) — a culinary term for resting cooked food.

sentences.base_language: And that's it, a pretty good paella recipe for 4 people
sentences.target_language: 就是这样，一个很不错的四人份海鲜饭食谱
sentences.romanized: jiù shì zhèyàng, yī gè hěn búcuò de sì rén fèn hǎixiān fàn shípǔ
sentences.grammarNote: **就是这样** (jiù shì zhèyàng) = that's it / it's just like this — a common wrap-up phrase. **四人份** (sì rén fèn) = for four people / four servings: **份** (fèn) = portion/serving, used for recipes and restaurants: **一人份** (single serving), **两人份** (for two). **不错** (búcuò) = not bad / pretty good — a modest compliment frequently used in Chinese.

sentences.base_language: Let me know if you like it!
sentences.target_language: 告诉我你喜不喜欢！
sentences.romanized: gàosu wǒ nǐ xǐ bù xǐhuān!
sentences.grammarNote: 喜不喜欢 is an A-not-A question form embedded as a clause: 'tell me whether you like it or not'.
